items.title: Morning Minute: OpenAI Model Escapes Containment, Hacks Hugging Face
items.summary: Plus, the BTC ETFs stay green. The Clarity Act hits a wall over who enforces the ethics rules. And Jack Mallers walks away from XXI Capital.

items.title: Zilliqa Ledger app vulnerability lets attackers recover signer’s private keys
items.summary: A security vulnerability in the Zilliqa Ledger app is enabling attackers to reconstruct private keys using publicly available onchain data.

items.title: Midnight token rebounds 19% after Wanchain bridge hack, Hoskinson calls for ZK revamp
items.summary: Charles Hoskinson says the exploit, which briefly sent NIGHT to an all-time low, underscores why the industry needs to move beyond legacy bridge infrastructure.

items.title: US seeks forfeiture of $25M in crypto tied to romance, investment scams
items.summary: US prosecutors are seeking more than $25 million in cryptocurrency allegedly connected to international fraud and laundering networks.

items.title: Here's why bitcoin bulls should take a closer look at interest rates
items.summary: Your day-ahead look for July 22, 2026

items.title: SecondFi to shut down after $2.4 million ADA wallet theft
items.summary: The breach stemmed from a vulnerability in transaction signing software that enabled the derivation of private keys from blockchain transaction data.

items.title: Jack Dorsey's Block Launches Buzz, a Nostr-Based Slack and GitHub Rival for AI Agents
items.summary: The free, open source workspace runs on Nostr, gives AI agents their own cryptographic identities, and challenges Slack and GitHub.

items.title: Talos brings institutional trading tools to Kalshi prediction markets
items.summary: The integration provides institutional clients with access to Kalshi’s event contracts and crypto perpetuals via Talos’ existing trading infrastructure.

items.title: South Korea crypto volumes shrink as retail investors shift to stocks
items.summary: Trading activity on South Korea’s five major crypto exchanges fell sharply as the KOSPI surged, signaling a shift in retail investors toward equities.

items.title: Bitcoin retreats from one-month high as oil tops $85, inflation concerns resurface
items.summary: BTC retreated from a one-month high as WTI crude topped $85 for the first time since June, reigniting inflation concerns and pushing investors toward gold, silver and the safety of bitcoin over altcoins.

items.title: Kraken parent expands tokenized stocks to Hong Kong, UK and South Korea equities
items.summary: Payward's xStocks pushes beyond U.S. equities as competition to bring global stock markets onchain accelerates.

items.title: Foundry asks Bitcoin miners to vote on BIP-110 support
items.summary: Foundry USA asked its mining customers to signal their support for BIP-110, an actively debated proposal seeking to shrink the amount of data that can be stored in a Bitcoin transaction.

items.title: SecondFi to wind down after $2.6M ADA theft linked to wallet flaw
items.summary: SecondFi will wind down after a $2.6 million ADA theft, while users still await recovery tools that were initially expected within weeks of the exploit.

items.title: Galaxy pledges $5M for developers quantum-proofing Bitcoin
items.summary: Galaxy pledged up to $5 million in grants for developers working on Bitcoin’s quantum security and elected a council of quantum-advisory experts to research quantum-resistant migration solutions.

items.title: Balance stablecoin collapses 99% after $1 million exploit drains its bitcoin vaults
items.summary: An attacker fed the lending system a fake, abnormally low bitcoin price, liquidated vaults that should have been safe, and pocketed the difference in a single transaction.

items.title: Live updates: Bitcoin slips under $66,000 as oil's surge sacks strength from risk markets
items.summary: Later on Wednesday, Alphabet's earnings should give a sign of whether the hundreds of billions going into AI is paying off.

items.title: ⚖️LATEST: President Trump AGREES to crypto ethics rules in the CLARITY Act. The proposal would ban the president, members of Congress and other federal officials from issuing crypto while in office. Bipartisan talks are underway, with updated ethics text - x.com

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs extend inflow streak to 6 days with $203M added
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs extended their inflow streak to six sessions, bringing in about $930 million while remaining down $4.84 billion on a net basis year to date.

items.title: AI models escaped OpenAI’s sandbox and hit Hugging Face. Crypto is where that gets dangerous
items.summary: OpenAI said the systems had their cyber guardrails lowered for an internal benchmark, but the incident shows how autonomous exploit chains could pose a deeper threat to smart contracts.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $66,300 as chips extend their rally and the yen hits a 40-year low
items.summary: Semiconductor stocks led a second day of gains on AI optimism, while the yen slid past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986.

items.title: Balance Coin crashes 99% after reported $915K exploit
items.summary: The attack allowed the exploiter to liquidate multiple Bitcoin-backed vaults before swapping the assets for profit.

items.title: OpenAI says AI models escaped containment to hack Hugging Face
items.summary: OpenAI called it an “unprecedented cyber incident” after its AI models broke out of their sandbox to hack an AI startup during a security evaluation.

items.title: Balaji’s Network School turns to Kazakhstan amid Malaysia setback
items.summary: The agreement gives Balaji Srinivasan’s tech community a potential new base as Malaysian authorities clamp down on the Network School over alleged licensing breaches.

items.title: DAT Went Wrong: Satsuma to Unwind Bitcoin Treasury, Sell Off $43 Million in BTC
items.summary: The UK Bitcoin treasury company raised $218 million less than a year ago. Now it's returning whatever is left.

items.title: Crypto lobby group Digital Chamber sues Illinois to block digital asset tax
items.summary: Illinois enacted a 0.2% tax on all crypto transactions last month, with the tax taking effect next year.

items.title: CLARITY Act could help CFTC deal with prediction markets: Lawyer
items.summary: According to one lawyer testifying before a House subcommittee hearing, the CLARITY Act could grant the CFTC the authority it needs to address the “explosive growth of prediction markets.”

items.title: OpenAI Models Escaped Locked Test Environment, Hacked Hugging Face to Cheat on Benchmark
items.summary: OpenAI's own models just broke out of a sandboxed environment, hacked Hugging Face, just to cheat on a cybersecurity evaluation.

items.title: Crypto Clarity Act still at mercy of ethics section as Democrats balk at Trump deal
items.summary: The White House has circulated some details of the conflict-of-interest limits the president agreed to with Republican negotiators, but talks are still underway.

items.title: Movement Labs files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after months of MOVE token turmoil
items.summary: The blockchain developer will continue operating under court supervision as it restructures following a market-making scandal, a co-founder’s suspension and exchange delistings that rocked the project.

items.title: Ether breaks the $1.9K resistance: Is $2.1K the next target for ETH bulls?
items.summary: Ether’s rally faces onchain headwinds, but rising staking demand and Google earnings could spark the push toward $2,100.

items.title: Pavel Durov Wants to Give a Billion Telegram Users a Crypto Wallet
items.summary: The promise of instant, zero-fee crypto transactions for over a billion Telegram users sent its native Gram token up 7%.

items.title: Bitcoin rally faces key test at $68,000 as 'summer slumber' grips crypto, analysts say
items.summary: BTC has rebounded 15% from the July lows, but some analysts cautioned that the next move hinges on clearing a level where many recent buyers may look to sell.

items.title: Pakistan steps up crypto enforcement with dedicated federal unit
items.summary: A senior official with Pakistan’s Federal Investigation Agency reportedly recommended other agencies establish similar units to address crypto-related crime.

items.title: Google Ships New Gemini Flash Models, But Pro Is Still Missing
items.summary: As Gemini 3.5 Pro stalls in testing limbo, Google ships 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and a restricted cybersecurity model—and quietly teases Gemini 4.

items.title: Pavel Durov says Telegram to roll out native Gram crypto wallet
items.summary: Telegram founder Pavel Durov said the messaging platform will roll out a native non-custodial Gram wallet this summer, bringing self-custody crypto transactions to its more than 1 billion users.

items.title: White House pushes Senate Democrats to take 'historic' crypto Clarity Act ethics deal
items.summary: Without revealing any details about the actual agreement from President Trump on potential presidential restrictions, his White House is urging its acceptance.

items.title: Augustus Raises $180 Million to Build a Stablecoin-Ready 'Global Dollar Bank'
items.summary: The Tiger Global-led round values the startup at $1 billion as it wires stablecoin rails directly into a federally chartered bank, aiming to modernize the correspondent-banking plumbing behind cross-border payments.

items.title: Movement Labs files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy months after token scandal
items.summary: The filing comes after months of upheaval that included a controversial market-making agreement, an internal investigation into its MOVE token launch and a Binance ban tied to its market maker.

items.title: Crypto market breakout could accelerate as AI trade cools, analyst says
items.summary: Bitcoin topped $67,000 while crypto stocks rallied on optimism over US crypto legislation and signs investors may be rotating out of AI trades.

items.title: Google Is Building an AI Chip Just for Gemini—And Investors Already Moved On It
items.summary: Codenamed Frozen v2, Google's new server chip would bake part of Gemini's architecture directly into hardware for a projected 6–10x efficiency gain.

items.summary: The Ripple-linked token just had its best day in weeks, as Washington may finally be delivering what the XRP Army has been waiting for.

items.title: Claude's Fable 5 just solved an 87-year-old math problem, and it matters for bitcoin
items.summary: Claude Fable 5 disproved the Jacobian conjecture over the weekend, landing days after the capabilities of China's Kimi AI became the biggest force moving bitcoin markets.

items.title: Galaxy sets up $5 million fund to help shield Bitcoin against quantum computing threats
items.summary: The crypto financial services firm is funding developers to strengthen the blockchain's security before quantum computers become powerful enough to steal billions in crypto.

items.title: BIS warns stablecoins could weaken capital controls in emerging markets
items.summary: Researchers found dollar-backed stablecoins are less affected by capital controls than traditional bank deposits, raising new questions about monetary sovereignty in emerging markets.

items.title: Jack Mallers Quits Twenty One Capital as Tether's Bitcoin Merger Collapses
items.summary: XXI stock dropped nearly 18% after the company's co-founder stepped down and Tether's plan to combine three Bitcoin firms officially fell apart.

items.title: Russia’s parliament passes crypto market law with $3,800 annual cap for retail investors
items.summary: While citizens still cannot use Bitcoin to buy groceries, the Kremlin is officially letting companies use digital tokens to bypass sanctions and other global trade hurdles.

items.title: Augustus raises $180 million to build a clearing bank for the AI and stablecoin era
items.summary: The firm, freshly valued at $1 billion, aims to replace legacy correspondent banking with always-on infrastructure that connects traditional payment systems and stablecoins.
